channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Golf: Port Harcourt to host Nigerian National Finals
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Port Harcourt’s Python Golf Club will host the Nigerian National Finals of the International Pairs Golf Championship. Amateur golfers compete for a spot in
Read More: https://punchng.com/golf-port-harcourt-to-host-nigerian-national-finals/

item.title: Meridianbet partners UFC for Belgrade fight night
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Meridianbet becomes the official sports betting sponsor for UFC Fight Night Belgrade, securing exclusive rights for UFC’s debut in Serbia.
Read More: https://punchng.com/meridianbet-partners-ufc-for-belgrade-fight-night/

item.title: Wikki Tourists administrator tasks players, coaches on NPFL return
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Wikki Tourists’ new administrator charges players and coaches to secure an immediate return to the Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) for the 2026/27 s
Read More: https://punchng.com/wikki-tourists-administrator-tasks-players-coaches-on-npfl-return/

item.title: Oil block bidding: Inside Nigeria’s seven-hour transparency test
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s latest oil block bidding round saw 31 winners from 143 bidders. Discover the transparency and challenges in this seven-hour process.
Read More: https://punchng.com/oil-block-bidding-inside-nigerias-seven-hour-transparency-test/

item.title: MAN warns inflation won’t ease without reforms
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Manufacturers Association of Nigeria warns that Nigeria’s inflation won’t ease without structural reforms, urging government action to improve producti
Read More: https://punchng.com/man-warns-inflation-wont-ease-without-reforms-2/

item.title: Nigeria’s money supply hits N133.25tn despite tight monetary policy
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s broad money supply reached N133.25tn in June 2026, despite the CBN’s tight monetary policy. This increase in Nigeria money supply signals growing
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerias-money-supply-hits-n133-25tn-despite-tight-monetary-policy/

item.title: Private sector credit climbs to N83.3tn amid lending growth
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s private sector credit surged to N83.3tn in June 2026, a 2.8% rise. Discover how this lending growth impacts businesses and the economy.
Read More: https://punchng.com/private-sector-credit-climbs-to-n83-3tn-amid-lending-growth/

item.title: Chrisland graduates 327, celebrates 16 UTME high scorers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Chrisland Schools celebrates the graduation of 327 students and 16 high scorers in the 2026 UTME, including Eze Somtobena with 362. Discover their success!
Read More: https://punchng.com/chrisland-graduates-327-celebrates-16-utme-high-scorers/

item.title: Foundation trains 28 seed producers, distributes agro-inputs to boost farming
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A foundation trained 28 seed producers and distributed agro-inputs in Bauchi State to improve food production and strengthen climate resilience among small
Read More: https://punchng.com/foundation-trains-28-seed-producers-distributes-agro-inputs-to-boost-farming/

item.title: Police confirm mysterious death of five Abia family members
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Police in Abia State confirm the mysterious death of five family members in Umuimo, Osisioma Ngwa. Investigations are ongoing into the Abia family death.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-confirm-mysterious-death-of-five-abia-family-members/

item.title: How Project Breathe Clean Air Abuja is Transforming Health, Homes in the FCT
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Project Breathe Clean Air Abuja is proving that access to clean cooking energy can do far more than replace traditional fuels as it can improve public health, protect the environment

item.title: US launches 13th night of strikes as Iran warns of escalation in the Gulf
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Tohid Asadi reports from Tehran as the US launches strikes for the 13th consecutive night.

item.title: Manipal Health Seeks to Raise Up to $960 Million in India IPO
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Manipal Health Enterprises Ltd. is seeking to raise as much as 92.7 billion rupees ($960 million) in an initial public offering, continuing a run of sizable share sales in India.

item.title: ‘There’s no salary’: DRC health workers tell of hardship as Ebola spreads
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Health workers in the DRC battle a range of obstacles, from infection risk to angry mob attacks.

item.title: Beyond asphalt: Katsina’s infrastructure test
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Infrastructure is often reduced to concrete, asphalt and ceremonial ribbons. Yet its true value lies elsewhere: in the trader who reaches the market faster, the commuter who travels more safely, the investor who sees opportunity and the community that no longer feels forgotten. By that measure, Gove

item.title: When the judiciary becomes ultimate threat to democracy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The judiciary is often described as the last hope of the common man. Whenever disputes arise, citizens turn to the courts for redress. When the powerful seeks to oppress the weak, the latter finds refuge in the temple of justice. This enduring faith in the judiciary rests on the expectation that jud

item.title: Gains, lessons from Oyo students’ rescue
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Beyond the pain, political controversy and anxiety that surrounded the kidnapping and eventual rescue of the students and teachers in Oyo State, there are compelling reasons to be grateful. What began as a national tragedy ended in a manner that offers both relief and hope. The post Gains, lessons f

item.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour.

item.title: Police Removed Prominent Scientists from the ADA Meeting. Researchers Respond
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.the-scientist.com/police-removed-prominent-scientists-from-the-ada-meeting-researchers-respond-74607 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49030474 Points: 9 # Comments: 0

item.title: US court affirms release of pro-Palestine scholar as legal battle continues
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Appeal judges' decision to side with Georgetown's Badar Khan Suri against Trump sets up Supreme Court intervention.

item.title: The superpower hiding in plain sight
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Imagine the following scenario: The conference room held 200 people, but in that moment, it belonged to one woman. She wasn’t the most senior person there nor was she the most qualified. But when she stood to present her team’s proposal, something happened. Her voice was, and her conviction was pal

item.title: TikTok removes 4.8m contents in Nigeria in Q2 2026
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: TIKTOK says it removed more than 4.8 million videos in Nigeria during the first quarter of 2026 as it intensified enforcement against harmful content and strengthened Artificial Intelligence safety measures. The post TikTok removes 4.8m contents in Nigeria in Q2 2026 appeared first on Tribune Online

item.title: China’s Gold Imports Surge After International Prices Slump
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Chinese gold imports rose to a two-year high in June, underscoring resilient demand in the world’s biggest bullion market after a plunge in international prices.

item.title: Beyond the rescue of Oyo pupils and teachers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The excitement that accompanied the release of the school children and teachers kidnapped by suspected bandits in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, after about 56 days, is not surprising. The 45 hostages were abducted from Baptist Nursery and Primary School, Yawota; Community Grammar Schoo

item.title: 2027: Election battle goes beyond Nigeria’s borders
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: As Nigeria edges closer to the 2027 general election, a curious pattern has emerged. The contest for Aso Rock is no longer being fought solely on the streets of Lagos, in the town halls of Kano or across the creeks of the Niger Delta. Increasingly, it is being waged in the corridors of Washington, i

item.title: Chinese Stock Turnover Shrinks as Sentiment Cools on Tech Sector
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Chinese daily stock turnover dropped to the lowest level in over three months, as investors shun the nation’s technology sector.

item.title: Experts: Akwa Ibom, Enugu Investments in Aviation Will Expand Air Travel Market
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Chinedu Eze Experts in aviation industry are of the view that the investments in state-owned airport hubs by Akwa Ibom and Enugu states will likely expand air travel market, increase

item.title: Terrorism, kidnapping: DSS insists on death penalty
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) has announced plans to appeal the life imprisonment sentences handed to three convicted members of the Ansaru terrorist group over the abduction of schoolchildren and teachers in Oriire Local Government, Oyo State. The post Terrorism, kidnapping: DSS insists on

item.title: WTO Fish Fund to Support Nigeria, Others In Implementing Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Oluchi Chibuzor and Jessica Erobomhan The World Trade Organisation, (WTO) Fish Fund Steering Committee at its seventh meeting, has approved $2.2 million grants to 17 developing and least-developed country (LDC) WTO

item.title: IGP unveils 14-day operation against criminals in Benue
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Olatunji Disu, yesterday announced a special 14-day security operation to flush out criminal elements from identified flashpoints across Benue. The post IGP unveils 14-day operation against criminals in Benue appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Army backs Cross River’s ‘Unite Against Terror’ security summit
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigerian Army has pledged full support for the 2026 Cross River State Security Summit as senior commanders of the 13 Brigade met with the Central Planning Committee (CPC) ahead of the event, themed “Unite Against Terror.” The post Army backs Cross River’s ‘Unite Against Terror’ security summit

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy meets with far-right US activist Laura Loomer
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Pro-Israel Trump ally sits down with Ukrainian president as she says Russia is helping Iran kill US soldiers.

item.title: EFCC declares Abuja resident wanted over money laundering
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has declared a 36-year-old Abuja resident, Sunday Adejumo, wanted over his alleged involvement in criminal conspiracy and money laundering. The post EFCC declares Abuja resident wanted over money laundering appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: TSSCN chairman tasks tour operators on responsible tourism
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Chairman of the Tourism Sector Skills Council of Nigeria (TSSCN) and Special Adviser to the Akwa Ibom State Governor on Tourism, Mr. Nkereuwem Onung, has called on tour operators in Nigeria The post TSSCN chairman tasks tour operators on responsible tourism appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Says to Take Bold FX Steps Decisively as Needed
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Japanese authorities will take bold steps to counter moves in the currency market decisively as needed, and a US Treasury report issued overnight underscores a shared view that excessive currency moves are undesirable, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said.

item.title: Mike Waltz pressed over Al Jazeera video during congressional hearing
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Ted Lieu challenges UN Ambassador Mike Waltz after playing Al Jazeera footage of Iranian strikes in Jordan.

item.title: Trump to Take Up Manila’s Concerns With Xi, Philippines Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US President Donald Trump assured Philippine leader Ferdinand Marcos Jr. that he would take up Manila’s concerns when he meets with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the Southeast Asian nation said.

item.title: Femi Gbajabiamila: The Weight of Power, The Grace of Restraint
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: FACTFILE with Lanre Alfred Every country produces politicians but only a few produce custodians. Nigeria, unfortunately, has seen too many of the former and too few of the latter. I

item.title: Robertson: Blue Seal to Increase FDIs in Nigeria with Two More Chemical Manufacturing Plants
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: With 48 years experience working in the oil and gas and industrial sectors across six continents in multicultural environments, and managing multicultural employees with over 10 of those years spent

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: David Onyeri Following the discharge and acquittal of the former Minister of Petroleum Resources, Mrs Diezani Allison-Madueke by a UK court after she and other former key players and other

item.title: ASKY Expands Fleet Size with Two New Boeing 737 MAX8 Aircraft
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Stories by Chinedu Eze ASKY has marked a major milestone with the delivery of two brand-new Boeing 737 MAX 8 aircraft in July 2026, reinforcing its commitment to operational excellence

item.title: Trump imposes new double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: With existing 10 percent levies set to expire, Trump issues new tariffs on 60 countries under forced labour provision.

item.title: PHP/USD: Philippine Peso Falls to Fresh Record Low as Oil Advances
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Philippine peso dropped to a new record low as oil prices climbed, putting pressure on the central bank to step up its defense of the currency.

item.title: Macquarie’s First Woman CEO Departs With a $339 Million Stake
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Shemara Wikramanayake is stepping down as head of Macquarie Group Ltd. after leading the Australian financial conglomerate to bumper profits and a record high share price. For that she will be richly rewarded, making her one of the best-paid female bank executives in the world.

item.title: NUT’s demand for security beef-up around schools
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: The demand by the Nigeria Union of Teachers (NUT) for fortified security around schools and the full implementation of the Safe Schools Initiative nationwide is quite timely and important. The demand came on the heels of the recent release of the abducted pupils and teachers in Oriire Local Governme

item.title: Modi Seeks to Calm Anger in Rare Video as India Protests Widen
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a fresh bid to calm mounting anger over examination paper leaks as students prepared nationwide protests on Friday, with demands growing for the education minister to step down.

item.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

item.title: PBOC Adds Most Liquidity to Economy in Five Months to Aid Growth
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s central bank made its biggest addition of liquidity to the economy in five months via its medium-term lending facility to support growth ahead of its late July Politburo meeting and to accommodate government bond issuance.

item.title: Guinness Announces 57.3% Profit Increase, Declares Additional N7 Interim Dividend
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayode Tokede  Guinness Nigeria Plc has announced its unaudited financial results for the second quarter (Q2) ended June 30, 2026, delivering another quarter of strong financial performance. The company declared profit after

item.title: Tantalizers Assures Shareholders of Long-Term Sustainability
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayode Tokede  The board of Tantalizers Plc has assured shareholders that the company’s emerging growth trajectory was founded on strong operational foundations that would ensure sustainable long-term profitability and returns

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Director General of the National Institute for Hospitality and Tourism (NIHOTOUR), Aare Abisoye Fagade, has emphasised the importance of collaboration with stakeholders for the institute to fulfill its mission of producing skilled professionals The post NIHOTOUR needs stakeholder cooperation to

item.title: Analyst: voting against Iran war funding a tough sell for Congress
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US says it has spent more than $37.5 billion on the Iran war so far.

item.title: Israel bombs Gaza after displacement orders, kills two Palestinians
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Humanitarian situation in Gaza is 'desperate', UN official says, as Israeli violations of 'ceasefire' intensify.

item.title: Obasanjo Rebuts Osoba’s Claims, Defends 2003 Political Decisions
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Former President Olusegun Obasanjo has strongly rejected allegations by former Ogun State Governor Olusegun Osoba that he deceived leaders of the Alliance for Democracy (AD) ahead of the 2003 general election, insisting that his actions were driven by national unity rather than political manipulatio

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Duro Ikhazuagbe in Glasgow Team Nigeria will begin medals chase this morning inside the SEC Armadillo Hall of the Hydro Stadium here in Glasgow, Scotland with six of our para-powerlifters in

item.title: NTIFE 2026 to highlight FTAN’s drive for PPP, increased investment
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Ahead of the upcoming Nigeria Tourism Investors Forum and Exhibition (NTIFE) 2026, the Federation of Tourism Associations of Nigeria (FTAN) has announced that this year’s event will focus on promoting investment, partnerships, and the development of the tourism sector. The post NTIFE 2026 to highlig

item.title: UN envoy for Yemen ‘alarmed’ by Houthis’ Red Sea attacks, calls for talks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Hans Grundberg warns that further confrontation risks 'deepening the suffering of the Yemeni people'.

item.title: Iran war live: US completes 13th night of strikes on Iran
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Trump says he is considering a ‘massive attack’, as Iran's Araghchi says 'mindless aggression' won't help a deal.

item.title: Scheffler bounces back from Open putting woes with strong start in Minnesota
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Scottie Scheffler bounced back from his poor performance with his putter at last week's Open as he returned to PGA Tour action on Thursday to card a six-under par 65 at the 3M Open.

item.title: PFN urges Tinubu to reject anti-preaching bill
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Body says proposed law is unnecessary, targeted at Christians By Olayinka Ajayi LAGOS — The Pentecostal Fellowship of Nigeria (PFN) has urged President Bola Tinubu not to assent to the anti-preaching and hawking bill currently before the National Assembly, describing it as unnecessary and unrelated

item.title: Osun Election: I didn’t own house while serving as Osun governor — Aregbesola
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Shina Abubakar, Osogbo OSOGBO — National Secretary of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Rauf Aregbesola, has claimed that he did not own a personal house throughout his eight years as governor of Osun State. Aregbesola made the claim while addressing supporters in a video that circulated onl

item.title: CXMT’s Frenzy Ignites Hopes of Futher Hardware Rally in China
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Investors are looking to Monday’s trading debut of CXMT Corp. to help renew a rally in Chinese semiconductor stocks that has been on pause ahead of the memory giant’s initial public offering.

item.title: Igboho vows to flush out bandits from Kwara forests
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Launches Iru Ekun Security Network, says outfit will support security agencies By Demola Akinyemi, Ilorin ILORIN — Yoruba Nation activist, Sunday Adeyemo, popularly known as Sunday Igboho, has vowed that his Iru Ekun Security Network will reclaim forests in Kwara State from bandits and other crimina

item.title: Most FDA advisers say peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500 should be made available in the U.S. Here’s what happens next.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The FDA is deciding whether seven peptides can be legally compounded, potentially opening the door to broader use in Americans.

item.title: Lawan applauds Tinubu’s expansion of army to 12 Divisions
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Commends recruitment of 28,000 personnel, says move will improve response to security threats By Henry Umoru ABUJA — Former Senate President and Chairman of the Senate Committee on Defence, Senator Ahmad Lawan (APC, Yobe North), has commended President Bola Tinubu for expanding the Nigerian Army to

item.title: Nigerian onion traders seek Ghana’s intervention over detention of trucks
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Cynthia AloThe Regional Observatory of Onion in West and Central Africa, ORO/WCA, has appealed to the Government of Ghana to urgently intervene in the continued detention of five trucks loaded with Nigerian onions at Kotoku Market, warning that the development is causing heavy losses on farmers,

item.title: MSSN hails court judgment on right of Muslim female students to wear hijab in public schools in Oyo
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Muslim Students’ Society of Nigeria (MSSN), Oyo State Area Unit, has hailed what it described as a landmark judgment delivered by a high court sitting in Ibadan, granting constitutional right to Muslim female students to wear Islamic hijab in government-owned schools across Oyo State. According

item.title: Sudan: 80 Sudanese Leave Uganda On Voluntary Return Flight
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Dabanga] Amsterdam / Kampala -- The 11th voluntary return flight carrying Sudanese refugees from Uganda to Sudan departed on Thursday with 80 returnees on board. The flight travelled to Port Sudan Airport aboard a Tarco Aviation aircraft and was sponsored by Sudan's General Intelligence Service.

item.title: Sudan: 'U.S. Congress Should Pass New Bills On Sudan' - Human Rights Watch
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Dabanga] Washington -- The US Congress is currently considering two bills on Sudan: the Preventing External Aggression and Conflict Escalation (PEACE) in Sudan Act of 2026 in the Senate and US Engagement in Sudanese Peace Act in the House of Representatives. Human Rights Watch (HRW) asserts that "b

item.title: Nasarawa lawmaker raises alarm over killing of 32 persons in constituency
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Edwin Philip, Lafia A member of the Nasarawa State House of Assembly representing Udege/Loko Constituency, Hon. Onarigu Onah Kana, has raised the alarm over renewed violence in his constituency, alleging that at least 32 people have been killed in the last three months. Speaking under a matter of

item.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear.

item.title: Collapsed Benin–Sapele–Warri Road: End cycle of palliative repairs, Ugolor urges Oborevwori
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Demands cost of governor’s ordered emergency repairs By Egufe Yafugborhi ASABA — Civil society leader, Rev. David Ugolor, has urged Delta State Governor, Sheriff Oborevwori, to engage the Federal Government and neighbouring Edo State to end decades of temporary repairs on the collapsed Benin–S

item.title: NDIC promotes financial literacy, deposit protection in Ebonyi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Jeff Agbodo, Abakaliki The Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) has organised a financial literacy campaign for students in Ebonyi State to enhance their knowledge of personal finance and create awareness about the protection of bank deposits. The awareness programme was held for staff an

item.title: TMC reaffirms commitment to spiritual growth, says education central in Islam
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Muslim Congress (TMC), Oyo State chapter, has reaffirmed its commitment to educational development and spiritual growth with the successful organisation of its 2026 Qur'an Competition and Spelling Bee for secondary school students across the state. The post TMC reaffirms commitment to spiritual

item.title: Police Recover Stolen Hilux, Arrest Two Suspected Vehicle Thieves
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The police have recovered a stolen 2022 Toyota Hilux and arrested two suspected members of a vehicle theft syndicate after an intelligence-led operation across Plateau and Bauchi states. The Plateau State Police Command said the Hilux, with registration number 18D-35-FG, was stolen from its owner&#8

item.title: Team Nigeria Shines At Spectacular Glasgow 2026 Opening Ceremony, Sets Sights On Commonwealth Glory
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The 23rd Commonwealth Games officially began in spectacular fashion on Thursday night as Team Nigeria made a memorable entrance during a dazzling Opening Ceremony at Glasgow’s OVO Hydro Arena, setting the stage for what the National Sports Commission (NSC) hopes will be another successful campaign.

item.title: EFCC Arraigns Man Over Alleged N3.8m Property Fraud
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has arraigned Alhaji Muhammad Talake before the Borno State High Court in Maiduguri over an alleged N3.8 million property fraud. Talake appeared before Justice Aisha Kumaliya on Thursday on a two-count charge of obtaining by false pretence and crim

item.title: NSCDC Arrests Six Over Abuja Pipeline Vandalism, Fuel Theft
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC), Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Command, has arrested six suspected pipeline vandals accused of puncturing Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) pipelines and stealing Premium Motor Spirit (PMS) in Gwagwalada Area Council. The suspects were

item.description: Former President Goodluck Ebele Jonathan, has expressed his sense of fulfilment at the enactment of the Nigerian Oil and Gas Industry Content Development (NOGICD) Act, 2010, and the many benefits that have accrued to Nigeria at the national and community levels since the emergence of the Nigerian Co

item.title: Murray returns as Draper's coach for Mubadala DC Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Andy Murray and Jack Draper appear to be working together once again after the 24-year-old was forced to miss out on Wimbledon earlier this month.

item.title: CJC Resolves 70 Land Disputes In Imo
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Imo State Community Justice Centre (CJC) in Owerri, on Thursday said they have resolved 70 land disputes, out of the 185 cases handled during its one year pilot phase. This is even as the Hague Institute for Innovation of Law (HIIL), has announced that the centre will be formally handed over to

item.title: 'Good luck to Dirk...': Littler dazzles in Blackpool as 'frightening' form continues
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: If a player hit a nine-darter on the World Matchplay stage, that would usually go down as their highlight. That is just the tip of the iceberg for Luke Littler in 2026.

item.title: Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua to step aside as company pushes into robotaxis, robotics
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Shashua has been invited to take the chairman of the board seat.

item.title: EFCC Returns N108m Recovered From Employees To Company
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has handed over N108 million recovered during an investigation into an alleged fraud involving two former employees of Greenwich Planet Sun King Nigeria Limited. The funds were presented to the company in bank drafts on Wednesday by the EFCC’

item.title: Police Arrest Two Over N32m Goods Diversion
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has arrested two suspected members of a syndicate and recovered a truck loaded with 570 bags of guinea corn valued at about N32m. The suspects, Olawale Kareem, 40, and Ogunbiyi Monday, 46, were arrested by operatives of the Command Monitoring Unit, Oyo Operational Base.

item.title: ‘I deceived nobody,’ Obasanjo fires back at Osoba over 2003 political row
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Obasanjo said Osoba was not privy to several political consultations that preceded his decision to contest the presidency after his release from prison.

item.title: Tinubu Approves Expansion Of Nigerian Army To 12 Divisions
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, has approved the expansion of the Nigerian Army’s structure from eight to twelve divisions a landmark move to enhance the country’s security architecture and improve the operational effectiveness o

item.title: Trump says frozen Iranian assets will pay for damage to shipping
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that the US would begin using frozen Iranian assets to pay for ships and cargo damaged by strikes in and around the Gulf. The post Trump says frozen Iranian assets will pay for damage to shipping appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: 98.css
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://jdan.github.io/98.css/#status-bar Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49028927 Points: 257 # Comments: 56

item.title: Nigeria Reaffirms Support For Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has reaffirmed its longstanding support for the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic (SADR), reiterating its commitment to the principles of self-determination, decolonisation and the peaceful resolution of regional disputes in line with the African Union’s objectives. The p

item.title: Papers: Valuations of several Man Utd midfield targets revealed
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The top stories and transfer rumours from Friday's newspapers...

item.title: NBA: I’ll resign if evidence of election rigging emerges – Badejo-Okusanya
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Badejo-Okusanya dismissed allegations by some contestants and members of the legal profession that the 2026 NBA presidential election was rigged in her favour. The post NBA: I’ll resign if evidence of election rigging emerges – Badejo-Okusanya appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Modric signs new AC Milan deal
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: Modric joined the Italian heavyweights in 2025, scoring two goals in 37 matches last season. The post Modric signs new AC Milan deal appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Sanwo-Olu, Media Icons, Prominent Nigerians Pay Tributes To Late Jakande
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Lagos State governor, Mr. Babajide Sanwo-Olu, media icons and prominent Nigerians on Thursday paid glowing tributes to the late Alhaji Lateef Jakande, the first Civilian Governor of Lagos State and Pioneer President of the Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE) and commended him for his selfless service to

item.title: FG hails WOFAN, BUK partnership on practical agricultural training
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Minister of Education, Dr. Tunji Maruf Alausa, has commended the Women Farmers Advancement Network (WOFAN) and the Centre for Dryland Agriculture (CDA), Bayero University, Kano, for investing in practical agricultural education The post FG hails WOFAN, BUK partnership on practical agricultural t

item.title: Drivers hail Sanwo-Olu for swift response over abandoned refuse at Obalende Motor Parks
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Both drivers and commuters thanked Sanwo-Olu for saving them from health hazards by giving a marching order to the Lawma workers to clear the refuse at the parks.

item.title: Adeleke Suspends NURTW Activities in Osun Motor Parks
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Osun State governor, Ademola Adeleke, has ordered the immediate suspension of all activities of the National Union of Road Transport Workers (NURTW) in motor parks across the state as part of efforts to restore order and strengthen public security. The directive was issued on Thursday after a meetin

item.title: Ibadan: Higher Ground Mega Schools honours outstanding learners with scholarships
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: …graduates 50 pupils, students The Proprietress of Higher Ground Mega Schools, Ibadan, Oyo State, Mrs W.A. Owolabi, has urged graduating pupils and students of the school to uphold integrity, discipline and good character as they advance to the next stage of their academic journey, stressing that tr
